title: Benzoyl-m-nitroacetanilide as a Gravimetric Reagent for Beryllium
author: Nirmal K. Chaudhuri
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Small quantities of beryllium (2.0 to 3.0 mg) have been determined gravimetrically with benzoyl­-m-nitroacetanilide. The yellow complex is precipitated from solutions of pH 6.5 to 9.0 and weighed directly as Be(C 15 H 11 O 4 N 2 ) 2  containing 1.58% of the metal after drying at 120° or after ignition to oxide. Using thioglycolic acid, magnesium-EDTA and/or H 2 O 2  as masking agents separation from Co 2+ , Cu 2+ , Mn 2+ , Pb 2+  ,  Ni 2+ , Zn 2+ , Al 3+ , Cr 3+ , Fe 3+ , Ce 4+ , Th 4+ , TiO 2+ , VO 2+  and UO 2  2+ , have been effected. The relative standard deviation is only 0.27%. The method is also applicable to the determination of beryl­lium content of beryl.
